name: Presidio Child Abuse Case
description: The Presidio Child Abuse Case refers to allegations of a child abuse ring operating at the Presidio Army base in San Francisco during the 1980s. The claims, initially surfacing during a divorce custody battle involving Susan Schnierle, alleged widespread sexual abuse, satanic rituals, and even child murder involving military personnel and prominent figures. Proponents point to inconsistencies in official investigations, witness testimonies (some later recanted), and perceived cover-ups as evidence supporting the existence of a larger conspiracy. Mainstream investigations by the FBI and local authorities found no credible evidence to support the most sensational claims, concluding that the allegations were largely unfounded and likely stemming from false memories and suggestive questioning. Critics argue that the lack of physical evidence, the recantation of key testimonies, and the absence of corroborating evidence undermine the conspiracy theories surrounding the case. The case remains a controversial topic, fueled by documentaries and online discussions, despite the official conclusions.
subtitle: Alleged abuse ring at army base.

claims.claim: A child abuse ring operated at the Presidio Army base.
claims.evidence: Initial allegations from Susan Schnierle and later testimonies from alleged victims.
claims.criticism: FBI and local authorities found no credible evidence to support the claims. Many testimonies were recanted.

claims.claim: Satanic rituals and child murder were involved.
claims.evidence: Some testimonies included descriptions of satanic rituals and alleged child deaths.
claims.criticism: No physical evidence or corroborating witnesses have been found to support these claims. They are considered highly improbable by law enforcement.

claims.claim: The government covered up the abuse to protect high-ranking officials.
claims.evidence: Perceived inconsistencies in the investigation and alleged suppression of evidence.
claims.criticism: Official reports detail thorough investigations. The 'cover-up' narrative relies on speculation and interpretation of events.

keyFigures.name: Susan Schnierle
keyFigures.role: Initial accuser and central figure in the allegations.

keyFigures.name: Paul Bonacci
keyFigures.role: Alleged victim who provided detailed, but later recanted, testimony.

keyFigures.name: Floyd Brown
keyFigures.role: Conservative activist who promoted the conspiracy theory.

keyFigures.name: FBI Investigators
keyFigures.role: Conducted official investigations that debunked the most sensational claims.

mythologyConnections: References to Satanism and ritual abuse often draw on mythological and religious imagery.
The idea of a secret cabal controlling events resonates with mythological themes of hidden power structures.

timeline.event: Susan Schnierle makes initial allegations during a custody battle.
FBI and local authorities conduct investigations.
FBI closes the case, finding no credible evidence of widespread abuse.
The case continues to be discussed and debated online and in documentaries.

sources.author: Ofshe, Richard

sources.title: Ofshe, Richard
